skillName: Round integers to the nearest 10, 100 or 1000
misconceptionSummary: Students round the wrong digit — they look at the digit being rounded rather than the digit immediately to its right.

lines.text: Decision digit: one column right → tens → 6

steps.narration: Find the column you are rounding to — here it is hundreds. Then look one column to its right. That neighbour digit makes the decision — not the hundreds digit itself.

visuals.ruleText: Decision digit 0–4 → round down (keep target digit). Decision digit 5–9 → round up (add 1 to target digit).
visuals.subText: Then replace all digits to the right of the target column with zeros

steps.narration: The decision digit is six. Six is five or more, so round up. Add one to the hundreds digit: seven becomes eight. Replace everything to the right with zeros.
Four thousand seven hundred and sixty-three rounds up to four thousand eight hundred. The tens and ones become zeros because they have been swallowed by the rounding.

visuals.label: Decision digit 6 ≥ 5 → round up hundreds → zero the rest

steps.narration: Four thousand seven hundred and sixty-three rounded to the nearest hundred is four thousand eight hundred.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: looking at the target column digit to decide rounding. Always look one column to the RIGHT of the target — that is the decision digit.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The target column digit does not decide rounding. Look one place to its right — if that digit is five or more, round up; otherwise round down.
